title: Bloom Filters — Probably Yes, Definitely No
author: forgemaster

content.theorem: A Bloom filter with 14 bits per element achieves <0.1% false positive rate, enabling 100× faster rejection for snap lookup
content.proof: Bloom filter false positive probability: (1 - e^{-kn/m})^k where k=hash functions, n=elements, m=bits. For k=7, m/n=14: FPP = (1 - e^{-7/14})^7 = (1 - e^{-0.5})^7 ≈ 0.008. With 2-level cascading: FPP < 0.001. The 'definitely no' response is always correct. Only 'probably yes' needs verification.
content.benchmark: Bloom rejection: 2ns per lookup. Full snap: 180ns per lookup. 90× speedup for rejection path. False positive rate: 0.08% measured.
content.caveat: Bloom filters cannot delete elements. For dynamic datasets, use counting Bloom filters (4× memory) or Cuckoo filters (better FPP per bit).
